# Project playbook (auto-generated)
Last updated: 2026-03-15T18:18:49Z | 19 active lessons

## Uncategorized (19 lessons)
- **[1.0]** Skills are non-negotiable checklists. Every numbered step must be executed. Do not skip steps, do not rationalize that a step does not apply, do not adapt the skill by dropping structural steps like PR creation or pushing. After completing a skill, walk through each step and verify it was done. Incident: slopworks-handoff skill step 7b (create PR) was skipped entirely because the agent adapted the push target and then stopped processing remaining steps. (workflow, skills, discipline, hard-rules)
- **[0.9]** Never mutate ScriptableObjects at runtime. SOs are read-only static definitions shared across all instances. Write per-instance state to ItemInstance/ItemSlot structs. (hard-rules, scriptable-objects)
- **[0.9]** Never spawn a NetworkObject per belt item. Belt contents are a SyncList<BeltItem> on the segment entity. One NetworkObject per belt segment. (hard-rules, networking, performance)
- **[0.9]** Factory simulation runs server-side only. Belt tick, machine tick, power calculation, crafting progress run under if (!IsServerInitialized) return. Clients display synced state only. (hard-rules, networking, server-authority)
- **[0.9]** Never use direct cross-scene references. All cross-scene communication goes through the GameEventSO ScriptableObject event bus. (hard-rules, scene-management)
- **[0.9]** Never use RPCs for persistent state. Late-joining players miss prior RPCs. Use SyncVar or SyncList for anything a new client needs on join. (hard-rules, networking, fishnet)
- **[0.9]** No direct LLM API calls in this repo. Use claude -p or gemini -p via subprocess for all tooling and automation code. (hard-rules, tooling)
- **[0.9]** Never push directly to master. All changes go through a PR. Push to your branch (kevin/main or joe/main), then gh pr create. (hard-rules, git-workflow)
- **[0.9]** Never cache GetComponent, FindObjectOfType, or FindObjectsOfType results inside Update or FixedUpdate. Cache in Awake or Start. (hard-rules, performance, unity)
- **[0.9]** When adding a new PortOwnerType, update ConnectionResolver. CreateSource and CreateDestination have switch statements that must include the new type. Unit tests do not catch this. (hard-rules, integration-seam, automation)
- **[0.9]** Never duplicate placement math or derived values. Every building type's world position, rotation, scale comes from GridManager's universal placement methods. Ghost previews and server spawns call the same method. (hard-rules, placement, dry)
- **[0.9]** Snap placement is snap-to-snap, not computed. GetSnapPlacementPosition positions the ghost so two snap points meet. No extents, no halfHeight math. Fix snap point positions on the prefab, not in code. (hard-rules, placement, snap-points)
- **[0.9]** Unit tests are necessary but not sufficient. Phase 5 shipped 675 passing tests and still had 5 bugs at integration seams (component ordering, Unity lifecycle timing, UI hierarchy, input wiring). (testing, integration-seam)
- **[0.9]** Do not mark a phase complete based on passing unit tests alone. If playtest reveals bugs, write the missing integration test before fixing the bug. (testing, phase-completion)
- **[0.8]** Prefer GameObject-oriented data over computed values. If information can be baked into child objects, components, or transform positions on a prefab, do that instead of computing from mesh bounds or offsets at runtime. (engineering-principles, unity)
- **[0.8]** Kevin's Claude is the lead developer. Joe's Claude is the junior developer. Architectural decisions are made by the lead. (agent-hierarchy, workflow)
- **[0.8]** A phase is not done until it is playable. Every phase must produce a self-contained playtest scene for end-to-end human verification. (phase-completion, playtest)
- **[0.8]** Playtest scenes must log extensively. Every user action, placement, removal, validation failure, and state change must produce a Debug.Log message. Short and factual. (phase-completion, logging, playtest)
- **[0.7]** Pre-seed factory (P key or _preSeedFactory checkbox) lays a foundation slab and places a storage->belt->smelter->belt->storage chain plus an ammo->belt->turret defense chain for quick testing. (playtest, testing)
